what_manipulated: spatial or temporal precision of attentional selection
what_measured: correlation of feature-binding errors between color and identity reports
experiment: Units are human observers performing color-and-identity reporting on multi-feature stimuli under spatial or temporal selection tasks. Independent variable is attentional resolution (varied between conditions of high/low precision). Dependent measure is the correlation structure of errors (intrusions) across features. Analysis examines whether error patterns show independence (consistent with random sampling hypothesis) or dependence (consistent with conjunctive binding). Comparison is between prediction of independent random sampling versus conjunctive feature binding.

what_manipulated: dimensionality of target orientation (2D edge vs 3D scene-based)
what_measured: visual search efficiency and reaction time
experiment: Units are human observers performing visual search tasks. Independent variable is the type of orientation property available for search (image-based 2D orientation versus scene-based 3D object orientation) using simple convex block stimuli. Dependent measure is search performance (reaction time and accuracy). Comparison is between image-based and scene-based search efficiency. Analysis demonstrates that both types of orientation facilitate preattentive search with comparable effectiveness.

what_manipulated: social class (upper vs lower)
what_measured: unethical behavior and attitude toward greed
experiment: Seven studies using mixed methods: observational (driving violations), laboratory experiments (decision-making, taking goods, negotiation, cheating, endorsement of unethical behavior). Units are human participants stratified by socioeconomic status. Independent variable is class status. Dependent measures vary by study but include behavioral violations, choice outcomes, and attitudes. Mediator analysis examines attitudes toward greed. Comparison is between upper-class and lower-class participants on each outcome measure.

what_manipulated: certainty about reciprocal liking (high vs low vs uncertain)
experiment: Units are college women viewing Facebook profiles. Independent variable is information about male targets' liking (certain high, certain low, uncertain). Dependent measures are attraction ratings and self-reported thought frequency about targets. Comparison is across three conditions: reciprocation confirmed, no reciprocation, and reciprocation uncertain. Analysis tests reciprocity principle (H1) versus uncertainty benefit (H2).

what_manipulated: source of affective response (true cause vs apparent cause vs confusion)
experiment: Using the affect misattribution procedure (AMP), units are human observers making valence judgments of targets preceded by affective primes. Independent variable is the categorization of processing: direct affective response to true cause, affective response to apparent cause, or misattribution via source confusion. Dependent measure is the attitude judgment. Multinomial modeling decomposes response patterns into component processes. Analysis uses latent variable modeling to separate processes contributing to observed misattribution effects.

what_manipulated: video game content (prosocial vs neutral)
what_measured: helping behavior and accessibility of prosocial thoughts
experiment: Four experiments with units as human participants. Independent variable is exposure to prosocial or neutral game content. Dependent measures vary across studies: likelihood of helping after a mishap, willingness and time invested in assisting with subsequent experiments, intervention in a harassment situation, and accessibility of prosocial concepts. Mediator analysis tests activation of prosocial thoughts. Comparison is between prosocial and neutral game conditions across multiple helping outcomes.

what_manipulated: stimulus orientation (upright vs inverted) for sexualized vs non-sexualized people
experiment: Units are human observers recognizing faces or bodies of people in images. Independent variables are stimulus orientation (upright/inverted) and degree of sexualization. Dependent measure is recognition accuracy. Analysis compares inversion effects (performance decrease with inversion) across conditions. Prediction: inverted sexualized people should show reduced inversion effect (like objects) while sexualized women should show person-like (inverted) recognition patterns.

what_manipulated: letter prime (A vs F vs J) presented before achievement task
experiment: Three experiments across different contexts (two classroom, one laboratory) with units as undergraduate or graduate students. Independent variable is the evaluative letter prime (A or F or neutral J) presented as ostensible ID code. Dependent measure is performance on cognitive tasks (analogy test, anagram test). Experiments test whether letter exposure affects performance outside awareness. Analysis uses funneled debriefing and word-stem completion to assess consciousness. Comparison is across letter conditions while controlling for task type and participant population.

what_manipulated: target political ideology and race (liberal vs conservative; European American vs African American)
experiment: Three studies with units as participants from diverse political backgrounds. Study 1: observational examination of natural group stereotypes. Studies 2-3: factorial experiments manipulating target race and political attitudes independently. Dependent measures are impression ratings and prejudice. Analysis tests value-conflict hypothesis by examining whether liking depends on ideological similarity. Covariates include system justification and social-dominance orientation. Comparison between liberals and conservatives on their preferences for ideologically matched/mismatched targets.

what_manipulated: emotional expression (angry, happy, neutral) in face and body independently, then in face-body composites
experiment: Three experiments with units as human observers. Exp 1: within-subjects judgment of faces or bodies with varied emotional expressions; measure is approachability rating. Exp 2: within-subjects judgment of face-body composite images; measure is approachability. Exp 3: within-subjects recognition of body expressions paired with congruent/incongruent facial expressions. Analysis compares approachability across emotion types and tests which channel (face vs body) dominates composite judgments. Additional analysis examines how facial expression context affects body expression recognition.

what_manipulated: numerical magnitude (small number 1 vs large number 9) paired with timing task
experiment: Two experiments with units as human observers performing timing/reproduction tasks. Exp 1: participants reproduce duration of numbers; measure is duration of motor response (key-press). Exp 2: participants reproduce standard duration while paired with small or large number cues; measure is duration. Independent variable is numerical magnitude. Analysis compares reproduced durations across magnitude conditions. Comparison tests whether task-irrelevant numbers affect temporal representation, indicating shared magnitude processing.

what_manipulated: category size (many vs few alternative possibilities) for outcome grouping
experiment: Five experiments with units as human decision-makers. Independent variable is the size of category containing the target outcome (many versus few alternatives), manipulated while holding objective outcome probability constant. Dependent measures include probability judgments and wager amounts. Grouping criterion is held irrelevant to objective probability. Comparison tests whether category size biases risk perception despite identical objective probabilities across conditions.

what_manipulated: knowledge about a source's liking of targets (before vs after forming own evaluation)
what_measured: implicit and explicit attitudes toward liking source
experiment: Four experiments examining Transfer of Attitudes Recursively (TAR). Units are human observers learning about sources who like or dislike targets. Experiments test whether source valence transfers recursively to the source. Exp 1: baseline evidence for TAR regardless of target knowledge. Exp 2: tests whether prior knowledge about targets overrides TAR via cognitive balance. Exps 3-4: examine mediation by explicit inferences versus implicit association. Dependent measures include both explicit attitude ratings and implicit attitude measures. Analysis tests lower-order associative versus higher-order propositional processes.

what_manipulated: mindfulness meditation induction (meditation vs control)
experiment: Four studies (one correlational, three experimental) examining debiasing via mindfulness. Study 1: correlational measurement of trait mindfulness and resistance to sunk-cost bias. Studies 2a-2b: between-subjects experiments comparing meditation induction to control on sunk-cost bias task. Study 3: examination of mediating mechanisms. Dependent measure is sunk-cost bias magnitude (degree to which prior costs influence decisions). Mediator analysis tests temporal focus and state negative affect as mechanisms. Comparison across trait and state-level mindfulness manipulations.

what_manipulated: experiential category structure (distinct events vs grouped into categories)
what_measured: preference for category elimination and subjective progress
experiment: Units are consumer decision-makers evaluating sequences of positive or negative experiences. Independent variable is whether experiences can be construed as distinct events or grouped into categories. Dependent measures include preference for category elimination and reported subjective progress. Analysis examines whether consumers differentially prefer eliminating categories of positive versus negative experiences. Comparison tests whether experience categorization influences hedonic utility predictions.

what_manipulated: menstrual cycle phase (high vs low conception risk) and group category (race vs minimal group)
experiment: Two studies examining relationships between fertility and prejudice. Study 1: Black and White women categorized by implicit association of out-group with formidability; measured bias against out-group men. Study 2: replication in minimal-group paradigm without race cues. Independent variables are cycle phase and group membership. Dependent measure is bias (favorability, distance, or preference). Analysis tests whether fertility-bias association extends beyond racial groups and depends on formidability associations. Comparison across racial and minimal-group contexts.

what_manipulated: odd vs even number primes paired with target stimuli (faces, names)
experiment: Multiple experiments examining gender connotations of numerals. Units are human observers making gender judgments of stimuli (baby faces, foreign names). Independent variable is numerical cue paired with target (odd or even number). Dependent measure is gender judgment (male vs female attribution). Analysis tests whether logically irrelevant numerals influence gender categorization. Comparison across number type (odd/even), stimulus type (faces/names), and effect magnitude across studies.

what_manipulated: structural property of social relationships (hierarchy vs equality vs comparison) and organizational form (hierarchical vs egalitarian company)
what_measured: processing fluency, liking, learning accuracy, and decision ease
experiment: Five studies examining ease of processing of hierarchical relationships. Study 1: reaction times to identify hierarchical versus equal pairs. Study 2: memorization speed of hierarchy versus equality diagrams. Study 3: learning speed of asymmetric power, asymmetric friendship, or symmetric relationships. Study 4: decision difficulty in hierarchical versus egalitarian companies. Study 5: familiarity effects on hierarchy fluency. Dependent measures include reaction time, memorization speed, learning accuracy, and liking. Analysis tests whether processing fluency mediates preference for hierarchy.

what_manipulated: lifestyle and personality factors (university student vs administrator; procrastination; conscientiousness; extroversion)
what_measured: interpretation of temporally ambiguous statements (Moving Time vs Moving Ego perspective)
experiment: Three experiments examining how personality and lifestyle predict resolution of temporal ambiguity. Exp 1: comparison between university students and administrators on deictic temporal statement interpretation. Exp 2: examination of procrastination and conscientiousness as predictors. Exp 3: examination of extroversion as predictor. Units are human language interpreters. Independent variables are personality/demographic factors. Dependent measure is preference for Moving Time versus Moving Ego interpretation. Analysis uses group comparison and correlational methods.

what_manipulated: thermal condition (warm vs cold) via beverage temperature or ambient room temperature
what_measured: social proximity judgment, language concreteness, and relational focus
experiment: Three experiments examining grounding of abstract concepts in thermal experience. Exp 1: warm versus cold beverage manipulation. Exps 2-3: warm versus cold ambient conditions. Units are human participants in thermoregulation and judgment tasks. Dependent measures include judgment of social distance, analysis of language concreteness, and coding of relational versus individual focus. Analysis tests whether temperature induces systematic shifts in social cognition. Comparison across manipulation methods (beverage vs ambient) and behavioral measures.
